data.description:
Add a `session_log` ticket type so agents can store verbose, durable session
logs in the rebar store and surface them later via keyword search — without the
verbose bodies degrading the dependency-graph / health hot paths that run
constantly during the parallel-agent workflow.

## Context

rebar has one compile path (`reduce_all_tickets()` in `src/rebar/reducer/_api.py`)
with **no lazy-loading**: it folds the full body (description + every comment) of
every ticket into memory. That path backs `search`, `list`, `ready`,
`next_batch`, `validate`, and `deps`. A verbose log stored as an ordinary ticket
would therefore tax every graph/health operation. The design intent is that
`session_log` tickets are compiled from JSONL **only for query operations**
(`search` / `show` / the new recent-logs command) and are **excluded** from the
operations that compile all tickets to build the dependency graph and run
store-health checks.

Ticket types are enumerated in `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` (the
`enum`) and `src/rebar/_commands/composer.py` (`_TYPES`), with type-aware behavior
in `src/rebar/graph/_hierarchy.py` (tiers) and `src/rebar/_engine_support/gates.py`
(clarity heuristics). Jira sync has no per-type exclusion today but already filters
by status in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_differ.py`. All three
interfaces (CLI / MCP / library) funnel reads through the single shared impl in
`src/rebar/_engine_support/reads.py`, so one library function auto-flows to CLI and
MCP. Every compiled state carries `created_at` (ns) for recency ordering.

## Decisions (locked with the requester)

- **Query visibility — searchable, hidden from `list`.** Included in keyword
  `search` results and the recent-logs command + `show`; **excluded** from default
  `list` (surfaced only via explicit `--type=session_log`) and from all
  graph/health ops.
- **Content model — all three approaches in scope, sequenced.** (1) the type plus
  normal `create`/`edit`/`comment`; (2) a convenience capture helper; (3) an
  auto-session hook that creates/updates a log per agent session. The hook depends
  on the foundation.
- **Linking — non-blocking only.** No `blocks` / `depends_on` (and excluded from
  the dependency graph), but `relates_to` / `discovered_from` permitted so a log
  can reference the work it documents.
- **Gates & lifecycle — exempt, no transitions.** Skipped by
  `clarity_check` / `check_ac` / `quality_check` and by `validate`'s orphan/health
  checks; cannot be `claim`ed or `transition`ed. `show`, `comment`, and `edit`
  work as normal.
- **Title guidance — documented, not enforced.** Titles should carry a short
  summary of the work (not merely date / time / session id); this lives in the
  ticket docs but is never validated.

## Success Criteria

- A `session_log` ticket type exists end to end (schema, validation, all three
  interfaces) and never syncs to Jira.
- Session logs are discoverable via `search` keywords and a "last 5 session logs"
  command available from MCP, CLI, and the library; they do not appear in default
  `list`.
- Graph/health operations (`ready`, `next_batch`, `deps`, `validate`) are provably
  unaffected by the presence or size of session logs (benchmarked / asserted).
- Session logs are gate- and lifecycle-exempt, support non-blocking links only,
  and support `show` / `comment` / `edit`.
- A capture helper and an auto-session hook let agents record verbose logs without
  hand-assembling them, and the docs describe the title-summary convention.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `session_log` is a valid ticket type accepted by `create` across schema +
      `_TYPES`, and rejected nowhere it should be accepted.
- [ ] Session logs are excluded from `reduce_all_tickets()` for graph/health
      callers (`ready`, `next_batch`, `deps`, `validate`) and from default `list`,
      but included in `search` and `show`.
- [ ] `claim` / `transition` on a `session_log` are refused with a clear message;
      `blocks` / `depends_on` links are refused while `relates_to` /
      `discovered_from` succeed.
- [ ] `clarity_check`, `check_ac`, and `quality_check` treat `session_log` as
      exempt, and `validate` does not flag session logs as orphans/empty/etc.
- [ ] A "last 5 session logs" capability (ordered by `created_at` desc, default
      limit 5) is reachable identically from the library, the CLI, and an MCP tool
      with a documented `outputSchema`.
- [ ] `session_log` tickets are never emitted to Jira by `reconcile` (outbound
      filter + absent from the local→Jira type map) and never appear in
      `bridge_fsck` output.
- [ ] A capture helper and an auto-session hook can create/append a session log,
      and `CLAUDE.md` + `docs/` document the type, its exemptions, and the
      title-summary convention.
- [ ] Tests cover the type across all three interfaces and assert the graph/health
      performance isolation; full suite passes.
data.title: Add a session_log ticket type for verbose, queryable session logs
